terms.definition: Negative entropy; the process or quantity associated with order generation in a system.
Rapid, non-linear shift from one stable macro-state to another when critical thresholds are crossed.
Structure-preserving mapping between two formal systems; here, between physics and theology.
Framework treating physics and theology as dual projections of a single divinely ordered relational substrate.
Quantitative aggregation of multiple social metrics scaled 0–100 to estimate societal coherence.
Unobserved variable inferred to account for covariance among observed variables.
Statistical algorithm that endogenously detects multiple structural breakpoints in time-series regressions.
Loss of phase coherence between components of a quantum superposition, leading to emergent classical behavior.
